prose:
Fukui reactivity indices (most electrophilic site)

Method provenance
Property: condensed Fukui f+ index for the most electrophilic atom (C atom #2) in formaldehyde.
Level of theory: DFT, B3LYP/def2-TZVP (no density fitting) in PySCF 2.x.
Geometry provenance: fixed single-point on the user-supplied formaldehyde.xyz (no re-optimization).
Solvent / environment: gas phase (no implicit solvent).
Charge & multiplicity: neutral singlet parent (charge 0, M = 1); cation state charge +1, doublet; anion state charge –1, doublet.
Convergence & caveats: all three SCF calculations converged; integrity status "ok" and trustworthy = true.  The automated charge-conservation checks report sums of f+ and f– = 1.0000 (expected ≈ 1), flagged as informational warnings (reproduced verbatim below). No other warnings.
Live log: chemkit streamed the SCF and job progress to its standard <subcommand>_<timestamp>.out file in the working directory; it can be tailed for full details.

Result
The largest electrophilic Fukui index (f+) is 0.7083913 on the carbonyl carbon (atom 2).  Values for all atoms are available in the JSON output.

Warnings (verbatim)
• fukui_charge_conservation_f+: sum(f+) = 1.0000 (should be ~1.0; large drift suggests an N±1 state did not converge)
• fukui_charge_conservation_f-: sum(f-) = 1.0000 (should be ~1.0; large drift suggests an N±1 state did not converge)
